changes: OpenClaw shipped two same-day releases on 2026-04-08 UTC: v2026.4.7 at 02:12 UTC and v2026.4.8 at 05:59 UTC.
ZeroClaw also published v0.6.9 at 2026-04-08 06:11 UTC, keeping the lightweight Rust lane active.
The atlas dataset remains cleaner than last week: Hermes Agent is pinned to NousResearch/hermes-agent, while TinyClaw / TinyAGI and HiClaw now point to their correct official repos.
The latest OpenClaw showcase additions still hold as valid source-backed use-case expansion: device-control handoff, scheduled visual morning briefings, and accounting intake from email plus PDFs.
whyItMatters: This is no longer just a cleanup run: the category anchor itself moved again today, which is the highest-signal kind of ecosystem activity.
OpenClaw's release velocity strengthens the read that the reference product is still setting pace, not just holding incumbency.
ZeroClaw's fresh release reinforces that the lightweight Rust lane is still active, but it does not yet overturn the broader market map.
Repo canonicalization still matters for the hosted atlas because strategic monitoring is only useful if it points to the real official surfaces.
Net: this is meaningful but still incremental. Enough for a short operator digest, not enough to rewrite the taxonomy or executive view.
